mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS surprises fail to produce positive 20d returns in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, monetary tightening reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and slower credit growth. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, the 60-day forward return is positive in >60% of instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day returns is positive and exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings. This interaction effect produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days, with stronger predictive power than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines >50k month-over-month AND UNRATE rises >0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132926k and UNRATE at 5.2% as of 2005-03-01
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and UNRATE deteriorate simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are positive in >58% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is not significantly higher than the maximum of the individual series' ICs.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power, supporting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or months with above-trend INDPRO produce positive 5-day returns in <52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 2000k units signals weakening residential investment and future consumer wealth destruction through lower home equity. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates reduced discretionary spending and financial sector stress.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market deterioration
failure_condition: When HOUST < 2000k, 60-day forward returns are positive in >62% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST level and 60-day returns is positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary policy constraint that compresses equity risk premiums. The interaction of these two factors produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real rates tighten.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 192.0 and FEDFUNDS at 2.63% as of 2005-03-01
failure_condition: When both inflation accelerates and FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, 20-day forward returns are negative in <55% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is not significantly higher than either individual series' IC.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 100k month-over-month signals robust labor market health and rising consumer confidence. This always-on trend mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved earnings expectations and lower recession risk.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or months with PAYEMS growth >100k produce positive 5-day returns in <53% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) deceleration below 2% annualized signals late-cycle slowdown that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations. Combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 5.0%), this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in recession risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth annualized < 2.0% AND UNRATE > 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 10994.3B and UNRATE at 5.2% as of 2005-04-15
failure_condition: When both GDP growth is weak and UNRATE > 5.0%, 60-day forward returns are positive in >62% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is not significantly higher than the maximum of the individual series' ICs.
